name: Chocolate Raspberry Cake (Triple Layer)
content:
## Overview
A decadent three-layer chocolate raspberry cake featuring moist dark chocolate layers, a jammy raspberry compote filling, fudgy chocolate buttercream, and a glossy ganache drip topped with fresh raspberries.

## Why This Recipe Works
- Cooked raspberry filling replaces fresh berries between layers for more concentrated flavor and better structure
- All-in-one mixing method - no creaming or stand mixer required
- Simplified decoration with ganache drip and fresh raspberries

## Key Ingredients
- Dutch-process cocoa powder for deeper chocolate flavor
- Buttermilk tenderizes gluten and reacts with baking soda for rise
- Hot coffee blooms cocoa for deeper flavor
- Vegetable oil keeps cake soft even when chilled
- Cornstarch thickens raspberry filling without off-flavors

## Instructions

### 1. Bake Cake Layers
- Preheat oven to 350°F. Use three 8-inch round pans (greased, parchment-lined)
- Whisk dry ingredients (flour, cocoa, sugar, baking soda, baking powder, salt)
- Whisk wet ingredients (buttermilk, oil, eggs, vanilla); combine with dry
- Slowly stream in hot coffee - batter will be thin and runny (this is correct!)
- Bake: 20 min, rotate pans, +6 min until centers spring back
- Cool in pans 20 min, then invert onto racks

### 2. Make Raspberry Filling
- Simmer raspberries + sugar + 1 tbsp water over medium heat; mash gently
- Simmer 5 min, stirring often
- Mix cornstarch with remaining 2 tbsp water; stir into berries
- Cook 1-2 min until thickened and glossy
- Cool completely before using

### 3. Make Chocolate Frosting
- Beat butter + salt until creamy (~2 min)
- Sift in cocoa; mix on low
- Gradually add powdered sugar, alternating with cream
- Add Chambord (optional) and vanilla; beat 1 min until fluffy
- Reserve 1 cup in piping bag for dam

### 4. Assemble Cake
- Place first layer on cake stand
- Spread 1/4 cup frosting thinly, pipe buttercream dam around edge
- Add half the raspberry filling inside dam
- Repeat with second layer
- Add final layer bottom-side up for level top
- Frost entire cake, chill 1 hour

### 5. Make Ganache and Finish
- Microwave chocolate chips + heavy cream in 30-sec bursts, stirring until smooth
- Cool ganache to 90-95°F (barely warm)
- Pour over chilled cake, let it drip down sides
- Immediately top with fresh raspberries while ganache is tacky
- Chill 30 min to set before slicing

## Pro Tips
- Use cake strips to prevent domed layers
- Rotate pans at 20 min for even baking
- Always pipe a buttercream dam to prevent filling from oozing
- Add raspberries to warm ganache so they stick
- Chill cake fully before ganache to prevent pooling

## Make-Ahead and Storage
- Cake layers can be made 1 day ahead, wrapped at room temp
- Assembled cake keeps refrigerated 3-4 days
- Freezes well up to 2 months

name: Chocolate Cheesecake
content:
## Overview
A decadent, crack-free chocolate cheesecake with chocolate cookie crust, velvety chocolate filling, glossy ganache topping, and chocolate whipped cream.

## Why This Recipe Stands Out
- Real chocolate (not cocoa powder): bittersweet + semi-sweet bars melted into batter
- Crack-free without water bath: foil-cover + separate water pan method
- Make-ahead friendly: chills overnight, freezes beautifully up to 3 months

## Ingredients

### Crust
- 24 Oreos (270g) - use whole cookies, cream filling included
- 4 tbsp melted unsalted butter (56g)

### Filling
- 2 bars bittersweet chocolate (4 oz/113g each), chopped
- 1 bar semi-sweet chocolate (4 oz/113g), chopped
- 1/4 cup hot brewed coffee (60ml)
- 3 packages full-fat cream cheese (8 oz/227g each), room temperature
- 1 cup granulated sugar (200g)
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 1/4 cup sour cream (60g), room temperature
- 1 tbsp vanilla extract
- 4 large eggs, room temperature
- 3 tbsp all-purpose flour

### Ganache
- 1/2 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips (90g)
- 1/3 cup heavy cream (80mL)

### Chocolate Whipped Cream
- 1 cup heavy cream (240mL)
- 1/4 cup powdered sugar (30g)
- 1 tbsp natural unsweetened cocoa powder
- 1 tsp vanilla extract

## Crack-Free Baking Method

1. Foil covering hack: Cover springform pan tightly with foil for the first hour of baking to trap moisture
2. Pan of water on lower rack: Place a large dish of hot water on the bottom oven rack
3. Low and slow: Bake at 300°F
4. Do NOT use a thermometer - use the jiggle test instead

## Instructions

### 1. Crust
- Pulse Oreos in food processor until sand-like; drizzle in melted butter while pulsing
- Press into 9-inch springform pan, bottom + 1 inch up sides
- Bake at 350°F for 10 minutes; cool
- Reduce oven to 300°F

### 2. Melt Chocolate
- Combine chopped chocolate with hot coffee
- Microwave in 10-20 second bursts, stirring between, until fully melted

### 3. Make Batter
- Beat cream cheese on medium speed until creamy (~2 min)
- Add sugar and salt; mix 1 minute
- Add sour cream and vanilla; combine
- On medium-low speed, add eggs one at a time
- Add melted chocolate; mix until almost combined
- Sift in flour; mix on low until incorporated

### 4. Bake
- Pour batter into cooled crust; smooth top
- Cover tightly with foil
- Place on rimmed baking sheet on middle rack; hot water pan on bottom rack
- Bake at 300°F for 1 hour with foil on
- Remove foil; bake another 40-50 minutes
- Turn off oven; crack door open; cool 1 hour in oven
- Cool 1 hour at room temperature
- Refrigerate overnight (minimum)

### 5. Ganache
- Heat cream until steaming; pour over chocolate chips; stir until smooth
- Cool 5 minutes, then spread over cheesecake top

### 6. Chocolate Whipped Cream
- Whip heavy cream, powdered sugar, cocoa powder, and vanilla until stiff peaks
- Pipe ring or dollops around the edge

## Pro Tips
- Room-temperature ingredients are non-negotiable
- Cold cream cheese = lumpy batter that never smooths out
- Eggs: soak in warm water if cold

name: Carrot Cake with Walnuts
content:
## Overview
A moist, tender, and lightly spiced homemade carrot cake topped with rich cream cheese frosting.

## Ingredients

### For the Cake
- 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour (300g)
- 2 tsp baking powder
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1/2 tsp ground nutmeg
- 1 1/3 cup vegetable oil (320ml)
- 1 cup packed light brown sugar (220g)
- 1 cup granulated sugar (200g)
- 4 large eggs, room temperature
- 2 tsp vanilla extract (10mL)
- 3 cups grated carrots (~1 lb / 315g)
- 1 cup chopped pecans (120g) (optional)

### For the Cream Cheese Frosting
- 1 block (8 oz / 227g) cream cheese, room temperature (not tub-style!)
- 1 cup unsalted butter, softened (227g)
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 4-5 cups powdered sugar (480-600g)

## Instructions

### 1. Prepare Dry and Wet Mixtures
- Whisk together flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg
- In a separate bowl, whisk sugars, oil, eggs, and vanilla until well combined

### 2. Combine Batter
- Pour wet ingredients into dry; stir with rubber spatula until just a few streaks of flour remain
- Fold in grated carrots and pecans - do not overmix

### 3. Bake
- Divide batter evenly between two greased and parchment-lined 9-inch round cake pans
- Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 30-35 minutes
- Cool completely in pans on wire rack (~1 hour)

### 4. Make Frosting
- Beat softened butter until smooth (~1 min)
- Add cream cheese; beat until fluffy (~30 sec)
- Gradually add powdered sugar on low speed
- Add vanilla; beat on medium-low until spreadable (~1 min)

### 5. Assemble
- Place one layer top-side up on cake stand; spread 3/4 cup frosting
- Place second layer top-side down for a flat top
- Frost top and sides with remaining frosting
- Chill 30 minutes before slicing

## Pro Tips
- Measure flour accurately: use kitchen scale or spoon-and-level method
- Peel carrots before grating: peels can be bitter
- Grate carrots fresh: pre-shredded carrots are too thick and dry
- Do not overmix: stop as soon as flour is incorporated
- Chill before slicing for clean cuts

## Storage
- Refrigerate up to 1 week, covered
- Freeze unfrosted layers up to 2 months

name: Pumpkin Cake with Cream Cheese Frosting
content:
## Overview
This easy pumpkin cake is moist, tender, and ready to bake in just 10 minutes. Ideal for holidays, stays fresh for days, and pairs perfectly with homemade cream cheese frosting.

## Key Ingredients
- Use 100% pure pumpkin puree (not pie filling) - Libby's preferred
- Vegetable oil or neutral-flavored oil for long-lasting moisture
- Both baking powder and baking soda for even rise and fluffiness
- Cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg (freshly grated)
- Full-fat brick-style cream cheese for frosting (not tub spread)

## Instructions

### Pumpkin Cake
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Prep 9x13-inch pan
2. Sift dry ingredients: flour, baking powder, baking soda, spices, salt
3. Whisk wet ingredients: sugar, pumpkin, oil, eggs, vanilla until smooth
4. Combine: pour wet into dry; mix by hand until just combined
5. Bake 30 minutes or until toothpick has a few crumbs
6. Cool completely on wire rack

### Cream Cheese Frosting
1. Beat butter until smooth
2. Add cream cheese; beat on medium-low until combined (~2 min)
3. Add vanilla + salt; mix
4. On low speed, gradually add powdered sugar
5. Increase to medium; beat until light and fluffy (~1 min)
6. Spread over fully cooled cake

## Pro Tips
- Measure flour correctly: use scale (240g = 2 cups)
- Room temperature ingredients: eggs 30 min out or soak in warm water
- Avoid overmixing: mix by hand just until no dry streaks remain
- Cool completely before frosting

## Variations
- Add orange zest; swap nutmeg for cardamom
- Cupcakes: 24 cupcakes, bake 20 minutes
- Layer cake: two 8-inch pans, bake 25-30 minutes

## Storage
- Refrigerator: up to 5 days covered
- Freezer: up to 3 months

name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Cake
content:
## Overview
A decadent 3-layer chocolate peanut butter cake with moist chocolate layers, fluffy peanut butter buttercream frosting, rich chocolate ganache drip, and miniature peanut butter cups.

## Ingredients

### For the Cake
- 1 cup buttermilk (room temp, 240mL)
- 3 large eggs (room temp)
- 3/4 cup vegetable oil (180mL)
- 1 tbsp vanilla extract
- 1 cup unsweetened cocoa powder (100g)
- 2 3/4 cups all-purpose flour (330g)
- 2 3/4 cups granulated sugar (550g)
- 2 tsp baking soda
- 1 1/2 tsp baking powder
- 3/4 tsp salt
- 1 cup hot water or coffee (240mL)

### For the Peanut Butter Buttercream
- 1 1/2 cups unsalted butter, softened (340g)
- 1 1/4 cups creamy peanut butter (325g)
- 1 tbsp vanilla extract
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 5 cups powdered sugar (600g)
- 4-5 tbsp whole milk

### For the Ganache
- 4 oz dark chocolate, chopped (113g)
- 1/3 cup heavy cream (80mL)

### For Assembly
- 8 miniature peanut butter cups, halved

## Instructions

### 1. Make the Cake Batter
- Preheat oven to 350°F
- Whisk buttermilk, eggs, oil, and vanilla until smooth
- Sift cocoa powder, then whisk in flour, sugar, baking soda, baking powder, and salt
- Combine wet and dry ingredients (batter will be thick)
- Add hot water or coffee; whisk until smooth
- Divide among three 8-inch round cake pans

### 2. Bake the Cakes
- Place 2 pans on one rack, 1 on the other
- Bake 20 minutes, then swap racks
- Continue baking 10-15 minutes until centers spring back
- Cool in pans 15 minutes, then invert onto wire racks

### 3. Make the Peanut Butter Buttercream
- Beat softened butter and peanut butter on medium-high for 2-3 minutes
- Add vanilla and salt; mix
- Gradually add half the powdered sugar on low speed
- Add 4 tbsp milk, then remaining powdered sugar
- Beat 2 minutes until fluffy
- Transfer 1 cup to piping bag with star tip

### 4. Assemble the Cake
- Place first layer on stand; spread 3/4-1 cup frosting
- Repeat with second and third layers
- Frost top and sides completely
- Refrigerate 20 minutes to firm up

### 5. Make the Chocolate Ganache
- Heat cream until steaming; pour over chocolate
- Let sit 5 minutes; stir until smooth
- Cool 15 minutes, stirring occasionally

### 6. Apply Ganache and Decorate
- Pour ganache over cake top; spread to edges and drip down sides
- Pipe reserved buttercream around top edge
- Place peanut butter cup halves between dollops
- Refrigerate 30 minutes before slicing

## Pro Tips
- Use hot coffee instead of water to enhance chocolate flavor
- Weigh flour for accuracy
- Use creamy peanut butter only (Jif or Skippy)
- Cool cake layers completely before frosting

## Storage
- Refrigerator: up to 5 days covered
- Freezer: up to 3 months

name: Easy Pineapple Upside-Down Cake
content:
## Overview
A classic pineapple upside-down cake with caramelized pineapple and maraschino cherries.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 4.95/5
- Total Time: ~1 hour
- Servings: 8

## Instructions

### 1. Prepare the Topping
- Melt butter in a 9-inch round cake pan
- Add brown sugar and stir until melted and bubbly
- Arrange pineapple rings and maraschino cherries in the pan

### 2. Make the Cake Batter
- Preheat oven to 350°F
- Whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt
- In another bowl, cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy
- Add eggs one at a time, beating after each
- Add vanilla extract
- Alternate adding flour mixture and milk, beginning and ending with flour

### 3. Assemble and Bake
- Pour batter over the pineapple topping in the pan
- Bake 40-45 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean
- Cool in pan 5 minutes, then invert onto serving plate

## Pro Tips
- Use canned pineapple rings for consistent size and sweetness
- Do not overmix the batter
- Let cool 5 minutes before inverting - too long and caramel may stick

name: Raspberry Cheesecake
content:
## Overview
A creamy cheesecake with fresh raspberry swirl on a graham cracker crust.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 4.96/5
- Total Time: ~6 hours (including chilling)
- Servings: 8

## Ingredients

### Crust
- 2 cups graham cracker crumbs (240g)
- 3 tbsp granulated sugar
- 6 tbsp unsalted butter, melted (84g)

### Filling
- 4 packages full-fat cream cheese (8 oz/227g each), room temperature
- 1 cup granulated sugar (200g)
- 1/4 cup sour cream (60g)
- 1 tbsp vanilla extract
- 4 large eggs, room temperature
- 1/4 cup all-purpose flour

### Raspberry Swirl
- 1 1/2 cups fresh raspberries
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1 tbsp lemon juice

## Instructions

### 1. Crust
- Preheat oven to 350°F
- Mix graham cracker crumbs, sugar, and melted butter
- Press into bottom of 9-inch springform pan
- Bake 10 minutes; cool

### 2. Raspberry Swirl
- Simmer raspberries, sugar, and lemon juice over medium heat
- Mash and cook until thickened, about 10 minutes
- Strain through fine-mesh sieve to remove seeds; cool

### 3. Filling
- Beat cream cheese on medium speed until smooth (~2 min)
- Add sugar; beat until combined
- Add sour cream and vanilla; mix
- Add eggs one at a time, beating on low after each
- Sift in flour; mix on low until just incorporated

### 4. Assemble and Bake
- Pour half the batter into crust
- Dollop half the raspberry sauce on top; swirl with knife
- Add remaining batter; dollop and swirl remaining sauce
- Tap pan on counter to remove air bubbles
- Bake at 325°F for 55-65 minutes until edges are set and center jiggles slightly
- Turn off oven; crack door; cool 1 hour in oven
- Cool 1 hour at room temperature
- Refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight

## Pro Tips
- Room temperature ingredients are essential
- Do not overmix - excess air causes cracking
- Tap pan after pouring to pop air bubbles
- Cool gradually to prevent cracks

## Storage
- Refrigerate covered up to 4 days
- Freeze up to 3 months

name: Caramel Apple Cake
content:
## Overview
A rich, moist Caramel Apple Cake infused with warm spices and fresh apples. Features apple-packed cake layers coated in cinnamon buttercream and topped with homemade salted caramel sauce.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 4.99/5 (54 votes)
- Total Time: 1 hour 40 minutes
- Servings: 8

## Ingredients

### Cake Layers
- 3 cups (360g) all-purpose flour
- 1 1/2 tsp baking powder
- 1/2 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 2 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1/2 tsp freshly grated nutmeg
- 1 1/2 cups (300g) granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup (110g) packed light brown sugar
- 1 cup (240ml) vegetable oil
- 3 large eggs
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 3 cups (400g) chopped apples (peeled, cored, 1/4-inch pieces)

### Salted Caramel Topping
- 1/2 cup (110g) packed light brown sugar
- 2 tbsp unsalted butter, softened
- 1/4 cup (120ml) heavy cream
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 1/4 tsp salt

### Cinnamon Buttercream Frosting
- 3/4 cup (170g) unsalted butter, softened
- 1/2 tsp cinnamon
- 1 lb (450g) powdered sugar
- 1/3 cup (80ml) heavy cream
- 2 tsp vanilla extract

## Instructions

### 1. Prepare Cake Batter
- Preheat oven to 350°F
- Grease and line three 6-inch cake pans with parchment paper
- Whisk flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, baking powder, and baking soda
- In separate bowl, whisk sugars, oil, eggs, and vanilla
- Pour wet into dry; stir until almost combined
- Fold in chopped apples (batter will be very thick)

### 2. Bake Cake Layers
- Divide batter evenly among pans
- Bake 40-45 minutes until toothpick comes out clean
- Cool in pans 10 minutes, then transfer to wire rack

### 3. Make Salted Caramel Sauce
- Combine brown sugar, butter, and heavy cream in saucepan
- Bring to boil; boil 1 minute or until 230°F
- Remove from heat; whisk in vanilla and salt
- Cool completely

### 4. Prepare Cinnamon Buttercream
- Beat butter and cinnamon on medium speed ~30 seconds
- Gradually add powdered sugar, alternating with heavy cream
- Add vanilla; beat until fluffy (~1 minute)

### 5. Assemble the Cake
- Place first layer on stand; spread 1/2 cup frosting, drizzle 1 tbsp caramel
- Repeat with second layer
- Top with final layer; frost top and sides
- Chill 30 minutes to set frosting

### 6. Add Caramel Drip
- Spread cooled caramel over top, gently pushing toward edges
- Refrigerate at least 10 minutes to set

## Pro Tips
- Weigh flour for accuracy
- For 8-inch pans: use 2 pans, bake 35 minutes
- Best baking apples: Granny Smith, Honeycrisp, or Fuji
- Avoid over-mixing to prevent tough texture
- Limit caramel between layers to 1 tbsp per layer

## Storage
- Refrigerate covered up to 5 days
- Freeze up to 1 month

name: Lemon Blueberry Cake
content:
## Overview
A moist, refreshing lemon blueberry cake with tender crumb, zesty lemon, and sweet blueberry notes. Filled and frosted with two buttercreams: lemon-flavored and blueberry-reduction-infused.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 4.97/5 (1,131 votes)
- Total Time: 1h 40m
- Servings: 10 slices

## Ingredients

### For the Cake
- 1/2 cup whole milk (room temp)
- 1/2 cup sour cream (room temp)
- 3 egg whites (room temp)
- 3 tbsp fresh lemon juice
- 1 tbsp lemon zest
- 1 tbsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups all-purpose flour (240g)
- 1 2/3 cups granulated sugar (333g)
- 1 1/2 tsp baking powder
- 1/4 tsp kosher salt
- 3/4 cup unsalted butter (room temp)
- 1 1/3 cups fresh blueberries (180g)

### For Blueberry Reduction
- 1 1/2 cups fresh blueberries (200g)
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tbsp water

### For Lemon Buttercream
- 1 1/2 cups unsalted butter (room temp)
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 6 cups confectioners' sugar (720g)
- 3 tbsp fresh lemon juice

## Instructions

### 1. Prepare Cake Batter
- Preheat oven to 350°F
- Butter and flour three 6-inch round pans
- Whisk dry ingredients: flour, sugar, baking powder, salt
- Toss blueberries with 1 tbsp flour mixture + dash of lemon juice
- Mix wet ingredients: milk, sour cream, egg whites, lemon juice/zest, vanilla
- Cream butter until light (~2 min), add dry mix on low until sandy
- Slowly add wet mix until just combined; fold in blueberries by hand
- Divide evenly into pans; bake 25-30 min until centers spring back

### 2. Make Blueberry Reduction
- Cook blueberries, sugar, lemon juice, water over medium heat until burst
- Mash and simmer until reduced by half
- Strain through fine-mesh sieve; chill liquid

### 3. Make Lemon Buttercream
- Beat butter + salt until fluffy (~3 min)
- Gradually add confectioners' sugar on low
- Add lemon juice 1 tbsp at a time
- Split frosting: 3/4 cup + blueberry reduction = blueberry buttercream
- Remainder = lemon buttercream

### 4. Assemble and Decorate
- Pipe thick ring of lemon buttercream on edge of layer; fill center with blueberry buttercream
- Repeat for second layer
- Apply thin layer of frosting (naked cake style)
- Pipe lemon buttercream dollops around top edge
- Fill center with frozen blueberries
- Chill 20 min before slicing

## Pro Tips
- Toss berries in flour + lemon juice to prevent sinking
- Use damp cake strips for even baking
- Measure flour by weight
- Do not over-mix

## Nutrition (Per Slice)
- Calories: 936 kcal
- Carbs: 134g | Fat: 45g | Protein: 5g

name: Oreo Cheesecake
content:
## Overview
A rich and creamy Oreo cheesecake with an Oreo crust, Oreo-studded filling, and chocolate ganache topping.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 4.97/5
- Total Time: ~14 hours (including chilling)
- Servings: 12

## Ingredients

### Crust
- 24 Oreo cookies, finely crushed (about 2 cups)
- 4 tbsp unsalted butter, melted

### Filling
- 4 packages (8 oz each) cream cheese, room temperature
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1/4 cup sour cream
- 2 tbsp all-purpose flour
- 1 tbsp vanilla extract
- 4 large eggs, room temperature
- 12 Oreo cookies, roughly chopped

### Ganache Topping
- 1 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips
- 1/2 cup heavy cream

## Instructions

### 1. Crust
- Preheat oven to 350°F
- Mix crushed Oreos and melted butter
- Press into bottom of 9-inch springform pan
- Bake 10 minutes; cool

### 2. Filling
- Beat cream cheese and sugar until smooth
- Add sour cream, flour, and vanilla; mix
- Add eggs one at a time on low speed
- Fold in chopped Oreos by hand
- Pour into cooled crust

### 3. Bake
- Place springform pan on baking sheet
- Bake at 325°F for 55-65 minutes
- Edges should be set, center slightly jiggly
- Turn off oven; crack door; cool 1 hour
- Refrigerate at least 6 hours or overnight

### 4. Ganache
- Heat cream until steaming; pour over chocolate chips
- Let sit 2 minutes; stir until smooth
- Cool 10 minutes; pour over cheesecake
- Refrigerate to set

## Pro Tips
- Use regular Oreos, not Double Stuf
- Room temperature cream cheese is essential
- Do not overmix after adding eggs
- Cool gradually to prevent cracks

## Storage
- Refrigerate up to 5 days
- Freeze up to 3 months

name: Butterscotch Cake
content:
## Overview
A fluffy, tender 3-layer cake with butterscotch buttercream and a homemade butterscotch sauce drizzle.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Total Time: 1 hour 40 minutes
- Servings: 12
- Calories: 1,268 kcal per serving

## Ingredients

### For the Cake
- 3 1/4 cups all-purpose flour (390g)
- 3/4 tsp baking soda
- 1/2 tsp baking powder
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 3/4 cup unsalted butter, softened (170g)
- 1 1/2 cups packed light brown sugar (330g)
- 4 large eggs, room temperature
- 1 tbsp vanilla extract
- 1/3 cup sour cream, room temperature (80g)
- 1/4 cup vegetable oil (60ml)
- 1/2 cup milk, room temperature (120ml)

### For the Butterscotch Sauce
- 1/4 cup unsalted butter, cubed (56g)
- 1/2 cup packed light brown sugar (110g)
- 1/2 cup heavy cream (120ml)
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 1/2 tsp salt

### For the Frosting
- 2 cups unsalted butter, softened (454g)
- 1 tbsp vanilla extract
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 12 cups powdered sugar (1,440g)
- 2 tbsp heavy cream
- 3 tbsp butterscotch sauce

## Instructions

### Cake Layers
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease three 8-inch round cake pans
2. Whisk flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt
3. Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy (~3 min)
4. Add eggs one at a time, scraping bowl between additions
5. Add vanilla, sour cream, and oil on low speed
6. Alternate adding 1/3 flour mixture and 1/2 milk, repeating until incorporated
7. Divide among pans; bake 20-25 minutes
8. Cool 20 minutes in pans, invert onto wire rack

### Butterscotch Sauce
1. Combine all sauce ingredients in saucepan
2. Bring to boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally
3. Cook until light amber; remove from heat; cool completely

### Frosting
1. Beat butter, vanilla, and salt on medium-high (~5 min)
2. Gradually add powdered sugar on low
3. Add heavy cream as needed
4. Add 3 tbsp butterscotch sauce; beat until fluffy (~1 min)

### Assembly
1. Reserve 1 cup frosting in piping bag with star tip
2. Place first layer on stand; spread 3/4 cup frosting
3. Repeat with second layer
4. Top with final layer; frost top and sides
5. Drizzle remaining butterscotch sauce over top
6. Pipe decorations; chill 30 minutes before slicing

## Pro Tips
- Room temperature ingredients emulsify better
- Alternate flour and milk additions to avoid overdeveloping gluten
- Make sauce while cake bakes so both cool simultaneously
- Only 3 tbsp of sauce goes into frosting; rest is for drizzling

## Storage
- Room temperature (airtight): up to 5 days
- Frozen: up to 3 months

name: Lemon Cheesecake
content:
## Overview
A creamy, triple-layer lemon cheesecake with buttery graham cracker crust, lemon-infused cream cheese filling, and luscious lemon curd topping.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 4.94/5 (31 votes)
- Total Time: 6 hr 10 min
- Servings: 8

## Ingredients

### Crust
- 2 1/4 cups graham cracker crumbs (270g)
- 3 tbsp granulated sugar
- 6 tbsp unsalted butter, melted (84g)

### Filling
- 1 1/4 cup granulated sugar (250g)
- 2 tbsp lemon zest, packed (10g)
- 4 packages full-fat cream cheese, room temp (8 oz/227g each)
- 3 large eggs, room temperature
- 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 1/3 cup heavy cream, room temp (80ml)
- 1/4 cup fresh lemon juice (60ml)
- 1/2 cup prepared lemon curd (174g)

## Instructions

### Crust
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Wrap 9-inch springform pan with two layers of foil
2. Pulse graham cracker crumbs, sugar, and melted butter until wet sand texture
3. Press into bottom and 1 inch up sides; bake 10-15 min until golden; cool

### Filling
4. Reduce oven to 325°F
5. Blend sugar and lemon zest in food processor ~30 seconds
6. Beat cream cheese, salt, and lemon-sugar on medium-high until smooth (~2 min)
7. Add eggs one at a time on medium-low, beating between each
8. Add vanilla, heavy cream, and lemon juice; beat until just combined
9. Pour into cooled crust; place in roasting pan; pour boiling water halfway up sides
10. Bake 50 min until puffed and slightly jiggly
11. Turn off oven; prop door open; cool 1 hour inside oven
12. Chill 4 hours or overnight
13. Spread lemon curd on top; pipe whipped cream; garnish with lemon slices

## Pro Tips
- Room temperature ingredients are essential
- Do not overmix - excess air causes rising then cracking
- Tap pan on counter after pouring to pop air bubbles
- Never open oven door during baking
- Cool gradually - going straight from oven to fridge causes cracks
- Zest carefully - stop before reaching white pith (it is bitter)

## Storage
- Refrigerate covered up to 4 days
- Freeze up to 3 months (without curd/garnishes)

name: Gingerbread Cake
content:
## Overview
A classic holiday dessert - perfectly spiced with ginger, cinnamon, and cloves, rich with molasses, and incredibly moist and tender. Baked in a single 9x13-inch pan.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 4.99/5 (81 votes)
- Total Time: ~45 minutes
- Servings: 12
- Calories: 379 kcal per serving

## Ingredients

### Dry Ingredients
- 3 cups (360g) all-purpose flour
- 2 1/2 tsp baking powder
- 2 tsp ground ginger
- 1 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
- 3/4 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp ground cloves
- 1/4 tsp baking soda

### Wet Ingredients
- 1 cup (240ml/325g) unsulphured molasses (not blackstrap)
- 1 cup (240ml) hot or boiling water
- 1 cup (200g) granulated sugar
- 3/4 cup (170g) unsalted butter, melted
- 2 large eggs (room temperature)
- 1 tbsp vanilla extract

## Instructions

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter and flour a 9x13-inch pan
2. Whisk dry ingredients in large bowl
3. Dissolve molasses in hot water in medium bowl
4. Whisk in sugar and melted butter until smooth
5. Add eggs and vanilla; whisk until fully combined
6. Pour molasses mixture into flour mixture; stir until just combined - do not overmix
7. Pour into prepared pan; bake 30-35 minutes until center springs back
8. Cool completely in pan on wire rack
9. Serve with whipped cream and/or dust with confectioners' sugar

## Pro Tips
- Use unsulphured molasses - blackstrap is too bitter
- Hot water thins molasses so it blends smoothly
- No mixer required - mixed entirely by hand
- Frosting options: cream cheese frosting with lemon zest, or buttercream
- Layer cake variation: divide between two 9-inch round pans

## Storage
- Room temperature or fridge in airtight container
- Freeze up to 3 months

name: Turtle Cheesecake
content:
## Overview
Rich and creamy turtle cheesecake inspired by classic turtle chocolates. Features layers of chocolate, caramel, and pecans over a chocolate cookie crust.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 4.73/5 (126 votes)
- Total Time: ~6 hours
- Servings: 8
- Calories: 993 kcal

## Ingredients

### For the Crust
- 20 chocolate sandwich cookies (Oreos - not double-stuffed)
- 1/2 cup pecan halves
- 1/4 cup unsalted butter, melted

### For the Filling
- 4 x 8-ounce blocks cream cheese, room temperature
- 1 cup firmly packed light brown sugar (220g)
- 1/4 cup + 2 tsp all-purpose flour, divided (35g)
- 2 tbsp heavy cream
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 4 large eggs, room temperature
- 1/2 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips, melted and cooled
- 1/2 cup caramel sauce (120mL)
- 1/2 cup chopped pecans

### For the Topping
- 1 tbsp heavy whipping cream
- 2 tbsp semi-sweet chocolate chips
- 2 tbsp caramel sauce
- 2 tbsp chopped pecans

## Instructions

### 1. Prepare Crust
- Preheat oven to 325°F. Spray 9-inch springform pan
- Pulse cookies and pecans in food processor until finely ground
- Stir in melted butter; press into pan bottom
- Refrigerate at least 30 minutes

### 2. Make Filling
- Beat cream cheese and sugar until smooth and fluffy (2-3 min)
- Add 1/4 cup flour, cream, and vanilla; beat until smooth
- Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each

### 3. Create Layers
- Remove 1 cup of batter; stir in melted chocolate
- Whisk caramel with remaining 2 tsp flour; stir in chopped pecans
- Layer order: caramel mixture on crust, chocolate batter over caramel, plain batter on top

### 4. Bake (Water Bath Method)
- Place roasting pan on bottom rack, fill halfway with boiling water
- Place cheesecake on middle rack
- Bake 75-90 minutes until edges pull away and center wobbles
- Cover loosely with foil after 60 min if browning too quickly
- Cool on wire rack 1 hour; refrigerate uncovered at least 4 hours

### 5. Add Topping
- Heat cream 15-30 sec; add chocolate chips; stir until smooth
- Run knife around pan edge; remove sides
- Drizzle with melted chocolate and caramel; sprinkle with pecans

## Pro Tips
- Do NOT open oven while baking
- Room temperature cream cheese: leave out overnight or microwave 20 sec at 50% power
- Center should be slightly jiggly; it sets as it cools
- Cool gradually - do not rush into fridge
- Clean slices: run knife under hot water between cuts

## Storage
- Refrigerate covered up to 4 days
- Freeze up to 3 months (add toppings after thawing)

name: Christmas Tree Cake
content:
## Overview
A festive vanilla layer cake decorated with buttercream pine trees made from ice cream cones, creating a winter wonderland dessert.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 5/5 from 113 votes
- Total Time: 1 hour 15 minutes
- Servings: 8 slices
- Calories: 330 kcal per slice

## Ingredients

### For the Cake
- 1 2/3 cups all purpose flour (213g)
- 1 cup granulated sugar (200g)
- 1/4 tsp baking soda (1g)
- 1 tsp baking powder (3g)
- 1/4 tsp salt (1g)
- 3/4 cup unsalted butter (176g, room temperature)
- 3 egg whites
- 1/2 cup sour cream (120g)
- 1 tbsp vanilla (15mL)
- 1/2 cup whole milk (120mL)
- 1/2 tsp peppermint extract, optional

### For the Buttercream
- 1.5 lb confectioners sugar (680g)
- 1 lb unsalted butter (454g)
- 1-2 tbsp heavy whipping cream
- 1 tsp vanilla extract (5mL)
- 5 drops green food coloring for the trees

### For Assembly
- 12 ice cream cones

## Instructions

### For the Cake
1. Preheat oven to 340°F. Butter and flour three 6-inch pans
2. Sift dry ingredients together
3. Beat wet ingredients together in separate bowl
4. Add wet to dry and mix until combined (don't overbeat)
5. Divide evenly into pans
6. Bake 30-35 minutes until centers are set and springy
7. Cool in pans 4 minutes, then transfer to cooling rack

### For the Frosting
1. Cream butter until light and fluffy
2. Add sugar and mix on high
3. Add vanilla and salt
4. Add milk tablespoon by tablespoon for smoother consistency
5. Divide into two batches; add green food coloring to one batch

### For Assembly
1. Cut ice cream cones into different sizes with serrated knife
2. Cut cones in half for side decorations
3. Pipe star-shaped dollops using #30 tip onto cones (bottom to top)
4. Place halved cones around cake sides before piping
5. Stack cake layers with two-tone buttercream
6. Cover cake in white buttercream and smooth with bench scraper
7. Arrange piped trees on top of cake
8. Sift confectioners' sugar over entire cake for snow effect

## Pro Tips
- Pan sizes: double recipe for 8-inch pans, triple for 9-inch pans
- Sour cream substitute: whole milk yogurt
- Flat layers: use cake strips
- Two-tone frosting: fill one bag halfway with white, one with green, place both in third piping bag
- Generous dusting of powdered sugar adds realism

## Storage
- Room temperature: up to 3 days
- Refrigerate if using cream cheese frosting

name: Christmas Cake
content:
## Overview
A festive vanilla layer cake decorated with buttercream pine trees made from ice cream cones, creating a winter wonderland dessert. Same recipe as Christmas Tree Cake.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 5/5 from 113 votes
- Total Time: 1 hour 15 minutes
- Servings: 8 slices

## Note
The christmas-cake URL redirects to the same recipe as christmas-tree-cake on Preppy Kitchen. See Christmas Tree Cake entry for full details.

name: Key Lime Pie
content:
## Overview
A classic key lime pie with a buttery graham cracker crust and tangy, creamy key lime filling.

## Key Details
- Author: John Kanell
- Rating: 4.95/5
- Total Time: ~4 hours (including chilling)
- Servings: 8

## Ingredients

### Crust
- 1 1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs (180g)
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar (50g)
- 6 tbsp unsalted butter, melted (84g)

### Filling
- 4 large egg yolks
- 2 cans (14 oz each) sweetened condensed milk
- 3/4 cup fresh key lime juice (about 20-25 key limes)
- 1 tbsp key lime zest

### Topping
- 1 cup heavy whipping cream (240mL)
- 2 tbsp powdered sugar
- Lime slices for garnish

## Instructions

### 1. Crust
- Preheat oven to 350°F
- Mix graham cracker crumbs, sugar, and melted butter
- Press into 9-inch pie dish
- Bake 10 minutes until lightly golden; cool completely

### 2. Filling
- Whisk egg yolks until pale and thick (~3 min)
- Add sweetened condensed milk; whisk until smooth
- Add key lime juice and zest; whisk until combined
- Pour into cooled crust
- Bake at 350°F for 15-20 minutes until set but still jiggly in center
- Cool to room temperature
- Refrigerate at least 3 hours or overnight

### 3. Topping
- Whip heavy cream and powdered sugar until stiff peaks
- Pipe or spread over chilled pie
- Garnish with lime slices

## Pro Tips
- Use fresh key lime juice for best flavor (bottled works in a pinch)
- Do not overbake - filling will continue to set as it cools
- For cleaner slices, freeze pie 30 minutes before cutting
- Can use regular limes if key limes are unavailable

## Storage
- Refrigerate covered up to 4 days
- Do not freeze (filling becomes watery)
